Title: Genome-Wide Analysis of DREB Genes Identifies a Novel Salt Tolerance Gene in Wild Soybean (Glycine soja)

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Population genetic analyses show a strong selective sweep (~380 kb) surrounding DREB3b in domesticated soybean. The DREB3b^Ref allele is nearly fixed in improved cultivars and landraces, whereas DREB3b^39Del is enriched in wild soybean, indicating strong artificial selection on DREB3b^Ref during domestication, likely due to pleiotropic effects on flowering time, seed size, and plant height rather than salt tolerance per se.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Natural coding variation in DREB3b (DREB3b^39Del vs DREB3b^Ref) is significantly associated with differences in salt tolerance index among soybean accessions.
2. The DREB3b^39Del allele, originating from wild soybean, is associated with higher salt tolerance than the DREB3b^Ref allele in the 424-accession panel.
3. Overexpression of DREB3b^39Del in transgenic soybean hairy roots enhances root growth under salt stress more than overexpression of DREB3b^Ref, demonstrating a stronger positive effect on salt tolerance.
4. Both DREB3b^Ref and DREB3b^39Del up-regulate positive regulators of salt tolerance (SIN1, WRKY27, WRKY54) and down-regulate the negative regulator NAC29, with DREB3b^39Del producing larger expression changes under NaCl treatment.
5. DREB3b^Ref shows clear signatures of artificial selection during domestication, becoming nearly fixed in cultivated soybean, while DREB3b^39Del remains enriched in wild soybean.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of W82 seedlings under water vs 200 mM NaCl to identify differentially expressed genes including DREB3b (BioProject PRJNA797227); resequencing data of 1295 soybean accessions (NCBI PRJNA394629; GSA PRJCA000205 and PRJCA001691) used for DREB3b haplotype and diversity analyses; expression profiles of soybean DREB genes in different tissues retrieved from public RNA-seq/microarray databases (Phytozome/PLEXdb).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DREB3b functions as a positive regulator of salt tolerance in soybean, with the wild-derived DREB3b^39Del allele enhancing salt tolerance more strongly than the domesticated DREB3b^Ref allele; overexpression of DREB3b^39Del in soybean hairy roots significantly improves root growth under salt stress and more strongly induces positive regulators and represses a negative regulator of salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: As a DREB/AP2/ERF transcription factor, DREB3b modulates transcription of downstream salt-tolerance regulators, with the DREB3b^39Del allele showing stronger activation of positive regulators (SIN1, WRKY27, WRKY54) and stronger repression of the negative regulator NAC29 compared with DREB3b^Ref under NaCl stress, thereby enhancing salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Salt stress–responsive transcriptional regulatory pathway in which DREB3b, a DREB/AP2/ERF transcription factor, up-regulates positive regulators of salt tolerance (SIN1, WRKY27, WRKY54) and down-regulates the negative regulator NAC29, thereby enhancing salt tolerance; the DREB3b^39Del allele has a stronger regulatory impact on these targets under NaCl treatment than DREB3b^Ref.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: DREB3b --(transcriptional activation)--> SIN1, WRKY27, WRKY54 (positive regulators of salt tolerance); DREB3b --(transcriptional repression)--> NAC29 (negative regulator of salt tolerance); effects are observed both under mock and 100 mM NaCl, with stronger activation/repression by DREB3b^39Del than by DREB3b^Ref.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DREB subfamily of the APETALA2/ethylene-responsive factor (AP2/ERF) transcription factor superfamily.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: DREB3b transcript levels are up-regulated under 200 mM NaCl treatment in W82 seedlings, consistent between RNA-seq (W82-Water vs W82-NaCl) and qRT-PCR validation.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: A 6-bp deletion in the DREB3b coding sequence (DREB3b^39Del) that removes two amino acids relative to DREB3b^Ref; genic (coding region); affects_function (accessions carrying DREB3b^39Del show higher salt tolerance index than those with DREB3b^Ref, and overexpression of DREB3b^39Del in soybean hairy roots confers stronger salt tolerance and stronger modulation of salt-tolerance regulators than DREB3b^Ref).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress imposed as 200 mM NaCl for 15 days on soybean seedlings for RNA-seq, expression, and salt tolerance index measurements, and 100 mM NaCl treatment for soybean hairy root growth and downstream gene expression assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Salt tolerance and domestication in soybean; identification of natural alleles and a core transcription factor underlying salt tolerance variation and artificial selection; development of molecular markers for salt-tolerance breeding.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt tolerance); plant architecture and yield-related traits (flowering time, plant height, seed size).
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: DREB3b^39Del, a 6-bp deletion allele in the coding region that removes two amino acids, is associated with significantly higher salt tolerance index than the reference DREB3b^Ref allele and confers stronger salt tolerance when overexpressed.
Plant_Genes.Superior_Haplotype: The DREB3b haplotype carrying the DREB3b^39Del coding allele (Hap. 2-derived) is enriched in wild soybean and is associated with higher salt tolerance index compared with haplotypes carrying DREB3b^Ref.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Relative to DREB3b^39Del, the DREB3b^Ref allele improves flowering time, increases seed size (100-grain weight), and decreases plant height, indicating pleiotropic effects on yield-related traits.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Soybean cultivar Williams 82 (W82) for RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, and hairy root transformation; wild soybean accession GDW013 as the source of the DREB3b^39Del allele; panel of 424 soybean accessions (85 wild soybeans, 153 landraces, 186 cultivars) for association analysis; panel of 1295 resequenced soybean accessions for haplotype and selection analyses; Agrobacterium rhizogenes strains for hairy root transformation; pCAMBIA3301 expression vector for overexpression constructs.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Williams 82 (W82) cultivated soybean; wild soybean accession GDW013 as the donor of DREB3b^39Del.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of soybean DREB genes using BLASTP against the Phytozome soybean genome; RNA-seq of W82 seedlings under water vs 200 mM NaCl to identify differentially expressed DREB genes; haplotype and association analysis of DREB3b variants with salt tolerance index in a 424-accession panel; nucleotide diversity and selective sweep analyses around DREB3b in 1295 resequenced accessions; Agrobacterium rhizogenes–mediated transformation to generate DREB3b^Ref- and DREB3b^39Del-overexpressing soybean hairy roots; GUS staining and qRT-PCR to confirm overexpression; hairy root phenotyping under 0 and 100 mM NaCl; qRT-PCR analysis of downstream salt-tolerance regulators (SIN1, WRKY27, WRKY54, NAC29, WRKY12, SOS1); development and validation of an InDel PCR marker distinguishing DREB3b^39Del and DREB3b^Ref alleles.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression of DREB3b^Ref and DREB3b^39Del coding sequences in transgenic soybean hairy roots via Agrobacterium rhizogenes–mediated transformation, followed by phenotypic analysis of root growth under 0 and 100 mM NaCl and qRT-PCR measurement of downstream salt-tolerance genes; combined with natural-variation association analysis of DREB3b haplotypes and salt tolerance index in 424 soybean accessions.
Plant_Genes.Genetic_Population: A diversity panel of 424 soybean accessions (85 wild soybeans, 153 landraces, 186 cultivars) used for association between DREB3b haplotypes and salt tolerance index; a resequenced panel of 1295 accessions (146 wild soybeans, 575 landraces, 574 improved cultivars) used for DREB3b haplotype frequency, nucleotide diversity, Tajima's D, and selective sweep analyses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: DREB3b was first identified as an up-regulated DREB gene under 200 mM NaCl via RNA-seq in W82, then prioritized based on natural variation and significant association between its haplotypes and salt tolerance index in a 424-accession panel. Its coding sequences from W82 (DREB3b^Ref) and wild soybean GDW013 (DREB3b^39Del) were PCR-amplified and cloned into the pCAMBIA3301 plant expression vector for functional analysis in transgenic soybean hairy roots. Population genetic analyses of 1295 resequenced accessions were used to assess selection around the DREB3b locus.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: DREB3b^39Del is a favorable natural allele from wild soybean that enhances salt tolerance and can be introgressed into cultivated soybean varieties. The functional InDel marker (100 bp for DREB3b^39Del, 106 bp for DREB3b^Ref) enables rapid genotyping and marker-assisted selection for the salt-tolerance allele in breeding programs. Understanding that DREB3b^Ref underwent strong artificial selection and is linked to flowering time, plant height, and seed size helps breeders design strategies to introduce DREB3b^39Del while maintaining desirable agronomic traits.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: DREB3a shows multiple haplotypes across soybean germplasm, with DREB3a^Ref being common in landraces and cultivars and DREB3a^167Ins present in a subset of lines with higher salt tolerance. Nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D analyses did not reveal strong evidence of selection around DREB3a during domestication, in contrast to DREB3b.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. DREB3a (Glyma.01G147600) is an up-regulated DREB transcription factor under salt stress in W82 seedlings.
2. Natural coding variation in DREB3a, notably the DREB3a^167Ins allele (15-bp insertion adding 5 amino acids), is significantly associated with higher salt tolerance index compared with the DREB3a^Ref allele in a 424-accession panel.
3. Haplotype analysis indicates that DREB3a haplotypes derive from an ancestral legume-like Hap. 2, but population genetic analyses do not show strong selective sweep around the DREB3a locus during domestication.
4. DREB3a is thus identified as an excellent candidate gene responding to salt stress and contributing to natural variation in salt tolerance, although no transgenic functional test was performed in this study.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of W82 seedlings under water vs 200 mM NaCl identified DREB3a as an up-regulated DREB gene; resequencing data of 1295 soybean accessions (PRJNA394629, PRJCA000205, PRJCA001691) used to define DREB3a haplotypes and local nucleotide diversity; publicly available expression data for soybean DREB genes retrieved from Phytozome/PLEXdb for tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DREB3a contributes to natural variation in salt tolerance in soybean, with the DREB3a^167Ins coding allele associated with higher salt tolerance index than the DREB3a^Ref allele, and DREB3a transcripts being induced by salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: DREB3a is a salt-induced DREB/AP2/ERF transcription factor likely acting at the transcriptional level to influence salt tolerance, as inferred from its induction under NaCl and association of its coding variant DREB3a^167Ins with increased salt tolerance index, although direct downstream targets were not examined.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Salt stress–responsive DREB/AP2/ERF transcription factor pathway; DREB3a is induced by salt stress and is implicated as a transcriptional regulator contributing to salt tolerance, although downstream targets were not characterized in this study.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DREB subfamily of the APETALA2/ethylene-responsive factor (AP2/ERF) transcription factor superfamily.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: DREB3a expression is up-regulated under 200 mM NaCl in W82 seedlings, as shown by qRT-PCR, consistent with RNA-seq data indicating induction by salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: A 15-bp insertion in the DREB3a coding sequence (DREB3a^167Ins) predicted to add five amino acids relative to DREB3a^Ref; genic (coding region); affects_function (accessions carrying DREB3a^167Ins show higher salt tolerance index than those with DREB3a^Ref).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl treatment for 15 days applied to W82 seedlings for RNA-seq and qRT-PCR to assess DREB3a expression under salt stress, and NaCl treatment used for phenotypic evaluation of salt tolerance index in the 424-accession panel.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Natural variation and domestication of salt tolerance in soybean; identification of DREB3a as a salt-responsive DREB transcription factor associated with salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: DREB3a^167Ins, a 15-bp insertion allele in the coding region predicted to insert five amino acids, is associated with a significantly higher salt tolerance index than the DREB3a^Ref allele in the soybean panel.
Plant_Genes.Superior_Haplotype: DREB3a haplotypes carrying the DREB3a^167Ins coding insertion (Hap. 2–4) are associated with higher salt tolerance index relative to haplotypes carrying DREB3a^Ref.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Soybean cultivar Williams 82 (W82) for RNA-seq and qRT-PCR; 424 soybean accessions (85 wild soybeans, 153 landraces, 186 cultivars) for haplotype and association analyses; 1295 resequenced soybean accessions for DREB3a haplotype and diversity analyses.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Williams 82 (W82) cultivated soybean used for expression analysis; multiple wild, landrace, and cultivated accessions used for haplotype and association studies.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification and classification of soybean DREB genes using the Phytozome genome and phylogenetic analysis; RNA-seq of W82 seedlings under water vs 200 mM NaCl to identify up-regulated DREB genes including DREB3a; haplotype and association analysis of DREB3a coding variation with salt tolerance index in 424 soybean accessions; qRT-PCR to validate salt-inducible expression; nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D analyses around DREB3a in a 1295-accession resequenced panel.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural-variation haplotype and association analysis linking DREB3a coding variants, especially DREB3a^167Ins, to salt tolerance index in a 424-accession panel, combined with qRT-PCR validation of DREB3a induction under 200 mM NaCl.
Plant_Genes.Genetic_Population: Panel of 424 soybean accessions (85 wild, 153 landraces, 186 cultivars) used to associate DREB3a haplotypes with salt tolerance index; panel of 1295 resequenced accessions (146 wild soybeans, 575 landraces, 574 improved cultivars) used to evaluate DREB3a haplotype origin and nucleotide diversity around the locus.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: DREB3a was identified as a member of the soybean DREB gene family and as one of the DREB genes up-regulated by 200 mM NaCl in W82 seedlings. Among 14 salt-induced DREB genes examined for natural variation, DREB3a showed significant association between its haplotypes and salt tolerance index in a 424-accession panel, highlighting it as a candidate salt tolerance gene.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because the DREB3a^167Ins allele is associated with higher salt tolerance index and DREB3a is salt inducible, this locus represents a potential target for marker development and selection of salt-tolerant soybean lines, complementing DREB3b-based strategies once further functional validation is available.
